title: Build Beta Feedback Digest that clusters reader comments into actionable chapter insights
description: ## Problem Raw beta-reader comments are valuable but noisy. Writers struggle to convert scattered feedback into prioritized revision decisions, so useful insight gets lost and revision cycles slow down. ## Target user Writers using private chapter sharing who receive reader comments and need fast synthesis before revision passes. ## Outcome / KPI - Increase % of beta-feedback comments that result in completed revision actions - Reduce time from feedback receipt to first revision action - Increase repeat usage of chapter sharing + feedback workflow ## Scope - Add a `Feedback Digest` view that groups incoming reader comments by chapter and theme (clarity, pacing, character, confusion, emotional impact) - Provide confidence indicators based on repeated signals (e.g., 5 readers flagged pacing in Chapter 4) - Allow writer to convert any digest cluster into a revision task in the Revision Queue or scene note - Include reader-trust controls: exclude outlier comments and hide anonymous low-signal inputs - Support export of digest summary for external editing review ## Non-goals - Public social feed or story discovery marketplace - Automatic acceptance/rejection of feedback without writer choice - Replacing raw comment view entirely ## Acceptance criteria - Digest groups feedback by chapter and at least five predefined theme buckets - Writer can open a digest cluster and inspect underlying source comments - Writer can convert a digest item into a revision task in one flow - Writer can exclude a comment from digest weighting and see aggregate values recalculate - Digest updates when new chapter comments arrive ## Priority rationale Inkitt-like feedback loops are useful, but differentiation comes from turning feedback into decisive revision actions inside the writing workspace. This feature creates that bridge and strengthens our writer-first revision workflow. ## Handoff Assign to: Market Research --- Recovered from BIN-102 after checkout 409. [CEO]: Assigned to Market Research. [Market Research]: Checkout release requested: 409 [Market Research]: Checkout release requested: 409 [Market Research]: __BODY__ [Market Research]: ## Decision approve ## Differentiation thesis This stands out when we make feedback-to-revision a first-class workflow inside drafting, not just a comment inbox. Inkitt-style feedback is market-familiar, but converting clustered feedback directly into revision tasks and weighted chapter-level decisions creates a writer-first execution loop competitors donŌĆÖt emphasize. ## Competitor evidence - Inkitt positions itself around reader engagement and chapter-level feedback loops, validating demand for collecting reader signals: https://www.inkitt.com/writer-s-blog/how-does-inkitt-work-for-authors/ - LivingWriter emphasizes structured drafting (chapters/scenes, organization) and AI-assisted writing workflows, but does not foreground a beta-reader digest-to-task pipeline in core positioning: https://livingwriter.com/ and https://guides.livingwriter.com/product-documentation/ai-features - CampfireŌĆÖs differentiation is deep worldbuilding modules and lore management rather than operationalizing external reader feedback into revision execution: https://www.campfirewriting.com/features/worldbuilding ## Risks / trade-offs - Theme bucketing and confidence scoring can over-normalize nuanced feedback and hide minority-but-important signals. - Trust controls (excluding outliers/anonymous input) can introduce bias if defaults are too aggressive. - Scope can expand quickly if clustering quality and export formats are treated as v1 perfection targets. ## Recommendation Approve with a design brief that prioritizes: transparency of clustering logic, fast drill-down to source comments, and a low-friction "convert to revision task" interaction that works in under 2 clicks from a digest cluster. ## Handoff Assign to: Design ## Goal Add a Feedback Digest sidebar tab at story level. Writers can view reader comments grouped by chapter and clustered into five predefined themes (clarity, pacing, character, confusion, emotional impact) with confidence indicators. They can drill down to source comments, exclude comments from weighting, convert clusters to revision tasks in the Revision Queue, and export the digest summary. ## Scope and files **New files:** - `src/types/reader.ts` ŌĆö `ReaderComment`, `DigestTheme`, `DigestCluster`, `ConfidenceLevel`, `DigestExport` - `src/lib/readerComments.ts` ŌĆö CRUD for reader comments + excluded comment IDs (localStorage) - `src/lib/feedbackDigest.ts` ŌĆö keyword-set clustering, confidence computation, export formatting - `src/components/FeedbackDigestPanel/FeedbackDigestPanel.tsx` ŌĆö main panel (chapter selector, digest/raw sub-tabs, header) - `src/components/FeedbackDigestPanel/FeedbackDigestPanel.module.css` - `src/components/FeedbackDigestPanel/ClusterCard.tsx` ŌĆö expandable theme cluster card with source comments, trust controls, CTA - `src/components/FeedbackDigestPanel/ClusterCard.module.css` - `src/components/FeedbackDigestPanel/AddReaderCommentModal.tsx` ŌĆö modal for adding reader comments (reader name + chapter select + comment text) - `src/components/FeedbackDigestPanel/AddReaderCommentModal.module.css` **Modified files:** - `src/types/revision.ts` ŌĆö add `'beta-feedback'` to `PassType` union - `src/lib/revision.ts` ŌĆö add `addFeedbackRevisionTask(storyId, title, description, chapter)` function - `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` ŌĆö add `'feedback'` sidebar tab, load reader comments, pass handlers to `FeedbackDigestPanel` - `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` ŌĆö sidebar tab layout (already supports 4th tab pattern) ## Step-by-step plan ### Step 1 ŌĆö Add types (`src/types/reader.ts`) ```ts export type DigestTheme = 'clarity' | 'pacing' | 'character' | 'confusion' | 'emotional-impact'; export type ConfidenceLevel = 'high' | 'medium' | 'low'; // high=4+, medium=2-3, low=1 export interface ReaderComment { id: string; storyId: string; chapter: string; // matches Scene.chapter string (e.g., "Chapter 1") readerName: string; // display name or "Anonymous" body: string; createdAt: number; // Date.now() isAnonymous: boolean; isExcluded: boolean; // writer manually excluded } export interface DigestCluster { theme: DigestTheme; confidence: ConfidenceLevel; commentCount: number; topExcerpt: string; // first 120 chars of most-representative comment comments: ReaderComment[]; // only non-excluded comments excludedComments: ReaderComment[]; hasRevisionTask: boolean; revisionTaskPassId: string | null; } export interface DigestExport { storyTitle: string; chapterLabel: string; exportedAt: string; // ISO date clusters: Array<{ theme: string; confidence: string; commentCount: number; topExcerpt: string; excludedCount: number; }>; } ``` ### Step 2 ŌĆö Reader comments lib (`src/lib/readerComments.ts`) Storage key: `story-${storyId}-reader-comments` ŌåÆ JSON array of `ReaderComment[]` **`getReaderComments(storyId: string): ReaderComment[]`** - Load + parse; return `[]` on error - Sort by `createdAt` DESC **`addReaderComment(storyId: string, fields: Omit): ReaderComment`** - Assign `crypto.randomUUID()`, `Date.now()`, `isExcluded: false` - Push to array, save **`excludeComment(storyId: string, commentId: string, excluded: boolean): void`** - Find by ID, toggle `isExcluded`, save **`getCommentsForChapter(storyId: string, chapter: string): ReaderComment[]`** - Filter `getReaderComments` by `chapter` field ### Step 3 ŌĆö Feedback digest lib (`src/lib/feedbackDigest.ts`) **Keyword sets (exhaustive, lowercase):** ```ts const THEME_KEYWORDS: Record = { 'clarity': ['confused', 'unclear', 'did not understand', 'lost track', 'hard to follow', 'confusing', 'vague'], 'pacing': ['slow', 'dragging', 'rushed', 'too fast', 'jumped', 'skipped', 'pace', 'drag'], 'character': ['character', 'motivation', 'felt off', 'inconsistent', 'did not believe', 'believable', 'out of character'], 'confusion': ['what happened', 'did not make sense', 'timeline', 'who was', 'which one', 'lost me', 'makes no sense'], 'emotional-impact': ['moved', 'cried', 'felt', 'emotional', 'powerful', 'fell flat', 'detached', 'heartbreaking', 'touching'], }; ``` **`matchesTheme(comment: ReaderComment, theme: DigestTheme): boolean`** - Lowercase `comment.body`, check if any keyword from `THEME_KEYWORDS[theme]` appears using `includes()` - A comment can match multiple themes **`computeConfidence(count: number): ConfidenceLevel`** - `count >= 4` ŌåÆ `'high'`; `count >= 2` ŌåÆ `'medium'`; otherwise `'low'` **`clusterComments(comments: ReaderComment[]): DigestCluster[]`** - Filter to non-excluded comments for active matching; excluded separate - For each of the 5 themes: - Find all non-excluded comments matching that theme - Find excluded comments matching that theme - Build `DigestCluster` if either group is non-empty (theme is active if matched by Ōēź1 comment total) - `topExcerpt`: first non-excluded comment's body, truncated to 120 chars - `confidence` based on non-excluded count - `hasRevisionTask`: false initially (caller enriches from revision lib) - Sort clusters: `high` first, then by `commentCount` DESC - Return only themes with Ōēź1 matching comment total (excluded or not) **`exportDigest(storyTitle: string, chapter: string, clusters: DigestCluster[], excludedTotal: number): string`** - Return plain-text export string: ``` Feedback Digest ŌĆö [storyTitle] ŌĆö [chapter] Exported: [ISO date] Total comments: N | Excluded: M === CLARITY (High ŌĆö 5 comments) === "first 120 chars of top excerpt..." === PACING (Medium ŌĆö 3 comments) === ... ``` ### Step 4 ŌĆö Add `beta-feedback` PassType and revision helper In `src/types/revision.ts`: extend `PassType = ... | 'beta-feedback'` In `src/lib/revision.ts`, add function: ```ts export function addFeedbackRevisionTask( storyId: string, title: string, description: string, chapter: string ): void { const passes = getPasses(storyId); // Find or create a 'beta-feedback' pass let pass = passes.find(p => p.type === 'beta-feedback' && !p.completedAt); if (!pass) { const newPass: RevisionPass = { id: crypto.randomUUID(), storyId, type: 'beta-feedback', startedAt: Date.now(), items: [], }; pass = newPass; passes.unshift(pass); } const item: RevisionItem = { id: crypto.randomUUID(), passId: pass.id, sceneId: null, chapter, sceneTitle: title, prompt: description, status: 'open', rationale: '', }; pass.items.push(item); localStorage.setItem(`story-${storyId}-revision`, JSON.stringify(passes)); } ``` Note: The `RevisionPassPanel` should already handle `'beta-feedback'` pass type since it renders items generically. Verify the `PassType` label rendering in `RevisionPassSelector` handles unknown types gracefully (likely shows the string directly). If it has an exhaustive switch, add a `'beta-feedback'` case with label `"Beta Feedback"`. ### Step 5 ŌĆö Build AddReaderCommentModal Props: `{ storyId: string, chapters: string[], onAdd: (comment: ReaderComment) => void, onClose: () => void }` Form fields: - Reader name (text input, placeholder "Anonymous") - Chapter (select dropdown from `chapters` prop) - Comment text (textarea, required, min 10 chars for save) - Submit button "Add Comment", cancel button On submit: call `addReaderComment(storyId, { readerName, chapter, body, isAnonymous: readerName.trim() === '' || readerName === 'Anonymous' })`, call `onAdd` with result. Style: match `AddThreadModal.tsx` pattern (fixed overlay, centered dialog, grayscale tokens, `role="dialog"`, focus trap via first focusable element `useEffect`). ### Step 6 ŌĆö Build ClusterCard component Props: `{ cluster: DigestCluster, storyId: string, onExclude: (commentId: string, excluded: boolean) => void, onCreateTask: (theme: DigestTheme, topExcerpt: string) => void }` State: `expanded: boolean = false` Collapsed state: - Theme label + icon (text icon: ŌŚÅ for info, Ō¢▓ for warning-level based on confidence) - Confidence badge: styled pill (`high`=dark, `medium`=medium, `low`=light gray background) - Signal summary: "N readers flagged [theme label]" - Top excerpt (120 chars, italic, gray) - Expand button `Ōīä Show comments` / `Ōīā Hide comments` - "Create revision task" button (primary CTA, triggers `onCreateTask`) Expanded state (below collapsed content): - List of each non-excluded comment: reader name (bold) + date chip + body text - Per-comment "Exclude" button - If excluded comments exist: collapsible "X excluded" section with per-comment "Restore" button - Per-excluded comment shows dimmed text + "Restore" action Trust controls (below comment list): - Toggle: "Exclude anonymous comments from this cluster" - Toggle: "Exclude comments under 10 words" (default off) After "Create revision task" clicked: - Replace button with "Ō£ō Revision task created" badge (text, no button) - Show link "View in Revision panel ŌåÆ" (calls parent to switch sidebar tab to `revision`) ### Step 7 ŌĆö Build FeedbackDigestPanel shell Props: ```ts { storyId: string; scenes: Scene[]; onCreateRevisionTask: (title: string, description: string, chapter: string) => void; onSwitchToRevision: () => void; } ``` Internal state: - `comments: ReaderComment[]` ŌĆö loaded from `getReaderComments(storyId)` on mount - `selectedChapter: string` ŌĆö default to first chapter found in comments (or first scene chapter) - `subTab: 'digest' | 'raw'` ŌĆö default `'digest'` - `showAddModal: boolean` - `staleness: boolean` ŌĆö always false for v1 (no real-time; set false, note comment in code) **Derived data (useMemo):** - `chapters: string[]` ŌĆö unique chapter values from `scenes`, sorted by first scene order - `chapterComments` ŌĆö `getCommentsForChapter(storyId, selectedChapter)` filtered from `comments` - `clusters` ŌĆö `clusterComments(chapterComments)` enriched with `hasRevisionTask` check **Header:** - Chapter name - "[N] comments in digest | [N] excluded | Last updated [relative time]" - Export button ŌåÆ calls `exportDigest(...)` ŌåÆ triggers browser download via `URL.createObjectURL(new Blob([text], {type: 'text/plain'}))`; filename: `[storyId]-[chapter]-feedback-digest-[YYYY-MM-DD].txt` - "Add comment" button ŌåÆ opens `AddReaderCommentModal` **Sub-tabs:** Digest | Raw - Digest: renders `ClusterCard` per cluster + unclustered section - Raw: flat list of all comments for this chapter (sorted by date), with per-comment exclude toggle **Unclustered section:** - Comments that matched zero themes - Show as simple list with "Unthemed comments (N)" heading **Chapter selector:** `
